stats.name: Tyrannosaurus Rex
stats.funFact: The T-Rex had one of the strongest bite forces of any terrestrial animal that has ever lived, estimated at up to 12,800 pounds of force.
stats.description: The Tyrannosaurus Rex is the undisputed king of the dinosaurs, a massive apex predator that dominated North America during the late Cretaceous period. Characterized by its immense skull, powerful jaws, and surprisingly small two-fingered arms, this theropod was built for pure lethality. Its keen sense of smell and binocular vision allowed it to track prey over great distances before launching a devastating ambush. Despite debates over its speed and scavenging habits, the T-Rex remains a symbol of prehistoric ferocity, leaving an indelible mark on natural history and capturing the human imagination like no other creature in the fossil record.

attacks.name: Apex Crush bite: 80, description: The user delivers a bone-shattering bite that ignores damage reduction.
Tail Smash damage: 50, description: The user swings its massive tail, potentially stunning the opponent.

passiveAbility.name: Intimidating Roar: Reduces the attack power of all enemies upon entry into battle.

stats.name: Triceratops
stats.funFact: Despite their massive skulls and horns, Triceratops had a beak similar to a parrot to help them slice through tough vegetation.
stats.description: The Triceratops is one of the most recognizable dinosaurs to ever roam the Earth during the Late Cretaceous period. Characterized by its massive bony frill and three distinct facial horns, this formidable herbivore used its physical assets to defend against apex predators like Tyrannosaurus rex. Living in large herds across western North America, they relied on their sheer size and collective strength for protection. This Fire-attuned variant possesses a blazing temperament, manifesting in heat-infused charges that can incinerate obstacles and ward off attackers with scorching displays of power, making it a legendary presence in any prehistoric landscape.

attacks.name: Blazing Gore Charge.description.A powerful frontal charge that leaves a trail of flames upon impact.damage.75
Inferno Frill Blast.description.The Triceratops heats up its neck frill to emit a burst of scorching energy.damage.50

passiveAbility.name: Molten Hide Armor.description.Reduces incoming damage by reflecting heat back at attackers upon physical contact.

stats.name: Brachiosaurus
stats.funFact: Unlike many other sauropods, Brachiosaurus had longer front legs than hind legs, giving it a giraffe-like posture that helped it reach the highest tree canopies.
stats.description: Brachiosaurus is an iconic giant sauropod known for its massive stature and elongated neck. This herbivorous titan grazed on high-foliage treetops that were unreachable by smaller dinosaurs. With its thick, pillar-like limbs and cavernous skull, it dominated the Jurassic landscapes. Despite its immense size, it possessed a surprisingly balanced grace. Modern paleontological theories suggest it held its head high to minimize energy expenditure while foraging for massive amounts of vegetation daily. As a legendary entity in this collection, it represents the absolute peak of evolutionary scale and ancient biological majesty, standing as a true monument to the Mesozoic era's grandeur.

attacks.description: The Brachiosaurus focuses its immense psychic energy to project a telekinetic shockwave that stuns opponents.
attacks.name: Mind Quake

attacks.description: Using its long neck to gain momentum, it delivers a massive physical strike empowered by psychic vibrations.
attacks.name: Astral Trample

passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming physical damage by 20% due to an impenetrable mental barrier surrounding its massive body.
passiveAbility.name: Mental Fortress

stats.name: Velociraptor
stats.funFact: Despite their movie portrayal, real Velociraptors were covered in feathers and were roughly the size of a turkey.
stats.description: The Velociraptor was a highly agile and intelligent predator that thrived in the arid environments of the Gobi Desert. Equipped with a distinctive, sickle-shaped claw on each hind foot, it was a lethal hunter that likely utilized pack tactics to take down larger prey. Its brain-to-body size ratio suggests it was one of the smartest dinosaurs of its time, possessing keen sensory perception. Though small in stature, its predatory efficiency and rapid reflexes made it a formidable terror of the Late Cretaceous, leaving an indelible mark on the history of evolutionary biology and prehistoric ecology.

stats.name: Ankylosaurus
stats.funFact: Its tail club was essentially a biological sledgehammer capable of shattering the bones of any predator that dared to attack.
stats.description: Ankylosaurus is the ultimate armored tank of the Cretaceous period. Covered in thick, bony osteoderms and sporting a massive, swing-ready tail club, this low-slung herbivore was nearly impervious to the sharpest teeth of its time. Living in lush, river-fed floodplains, it used its beak to graze on low-growing vegetation. Despite its intimidating, rugged appearance, it was a peaceful creature that only used its heavy armor and club for self-defense. Today, it remains an iconic symbol of evolutionary survival, representing the perfect balance between heavy defensive plating and devastating offensive capabilities in a prehistoric world filled with danger.

attacks.description: Swings the heavy tail club, dealing crushing impact damage to the target.
attacks.name: Tail Smash

attacks.description: Sprays a pressurized jet of water from its armored ridges to disorient foes.
attacks.name: Hydro-Spike

passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming damage by 20% due to the thick bony armor plates covering the body.
passiveAbility.name: Heavy Plating

stats.name: Spinosaurus
stats.funFact: The Spinosaurus is the largest known carnivorous dinosaur, even larger than the T-Rex, and possessed a unique sail-like structure on its back.
stats.description: Spinosaurus was a formidable predator that dominated the river systems of North Africa during the Cretaceous period. Distinctive for its massive dorsal sail and elongated, crocodilian-like snout, it was perfectly adapted for a semi-aquatic lifestyle. Its powerful jaws were equipped with conical teeth, ideal for catching slippery prehistoric fish. Beyond its aquatic prowess, its thick, dense bones allowed it to submerge efficiently while hunting. This massive carnivore represents one of the most enigmatic and spectacular discoveries in paleontology, shifting our understanding of how giant theropods interacted with their environments through specialized hunting tactics and evolutionary anatomical traits.

attacks.description: The Spinosaurus snaps its powerful, lightning-charged jaws onto the target, dealing massive electrical piercing damage.
attacks.name: Volt Crunch

attacks.description: The dinosaur splashes water charged with static electricity, shocking all enemies in a wide radius.
attacks.name: Static Tsunami

passiveAbility.description: Increases movement speed and damage output when battling in water-based environments.
passiveAbility.name: Conductive Swimmer

stats.name: Pteranodon
stats.funFact: Pteranodons were not technically dinosaurs, but rather flying reptiles called pterosaurs.
stats.description: Pteranodon was a master of the skies, possessing a massive wingspan that allowed it to soar over ancient oceans with ease. It lacked traditional teeth, instead utilizing a sharp, pointed beak to snatch fish from the water's surface. With its iconic cranial crest acting as a rudder, this reptile navigated complex wind currents during the Late Cretaceous period. While primarily a predator of the sea, its hollow bones made it incredibly lightweight. Today, Pteranodon remains a symbol of prehistoric flight, capturing the imagination of scientists and enthusiasts as a dominant aerial creature of its era.

attacks.description: The Pteranodon dives from high altitudes to strike its opponent with a swift, snapping beak attack.
attacks.name: Hydro Dive

attacks.description: A powerful wing gust that creates a localized water vortex to disorient and damage enemies.
attacks.name: Tidal Wing-Beat

passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming damage by 10 percent when the unit is above half health due to its agile flight maneuvers.
passiveAbility.name: Aerial Evasion

stats.name: Parasaurolophus
stats.funFact: Its iconic long, tubular head crest may have been used to produce low-frequency booming calls to communicate with other herd members over long distances.
stats.description: Parasaurolophus is a striking hadrosaurid dinosaur, easily identified by the elongated, curved crest extending backward from its skull. This herbivore thrived in the lush wetlands of the Late Cretaceous period. Research suggests the crest was hollow and connected to the nasal passages, potentially acting as a resonating chamber for vocalization or a cooling mechanism. With a duck-billed mouth for gathering vegetation and a powerful, quadrupedal stance, it was well-adapted for grazing. Though gentle in nature, its presence in large herds provided safety from predators, making it a social and resilient inhabitant of ancient North American floodplains.

attacks.description: The dinosaur uses its head crest to release a pressurized sonic blast of water.
attacks.name: Sonic Hydro-Blast

attacks.description: A heavy strike with its tail that pushes enemies back into deeper water.
attacks.name: Tail Slam

passiveAbility.description: Regenerates health points when standing in water tiles.
passiveAbility.name: Amphibious Recovery

stats.name: Allosaurus
stats.funFact: Allosaurus is often called the lion of the Jurassic because of its pack hunting behavior and apex predator status.
stats.description: Allosaurus was the dominant predator of the Late Jurassic period, easily recognized by the pair of distinct, horn-like crests sitting above its eyes. Possessing a lighter frame than the later Tyrannosaurus Rex, this bipedal carnivore utilized a unique ambush style to take down large sauropods. Its jaws featured serrated teeth designed for slicing through flesh, while its powerful, three-fingered claws allowed it to grapple with prey. As a highly successful evolutionary design, Allosaurus maintained its position at the top of the food chain, patrolling the vast floodplains of what is now North America with calculated, lethal efficiency.

attacks.description: The Allosaurus lunges forward, drenching the opponent in a pressurized jet of water before biting down hard.
attacks.name: Hydro-Bite

attacks.description: Uses its powerful tail to create a sweeping wave of water that destabilizes the enemy's stance.
attacks.name: Tidal Sweep

passiveAbility.description: Reduces damage taken from fire-based attacks by 50% due to the dinosaur's protective layer of aquatic energy.
passiveAbility.name: Mist Shield

stats.name: Apatosaurus
stats.funFact: Its tail was used like a bullwhip to create a sonic boom to deter predators.
stats.description: Apatosaurus was a massive, long-necked herbivore that dominated the floodplains of the Late Jurassic period. Characterized by its sturdy build and whip-like tail, this giant could reach impressive lengths and weights. As a sauropod, it spent most of its time grazing on lush vegetation, using its peg-like teeth to strip leaves from tall conifers. Despite its immense size, Apatosaurus likely lived in herds for protection against fearsome predators like Allosaurus. Its evolutionary design, featuring hollow bones to manage weight, allowed this gentle giant to navigate the prehistoric landscape effectively while maintaining a steady food intake to sustain its metabolism.

attacks.description: The dinosaur swings its massive tail in a wide arc, spraying surrounding water at the target.
attacks.name: Tail Torrent

attacks.description: A powerful stomping attack that creates a miniature tidal wave to crush opponents.
attacks.name: Titan Tsunami

passiveAbility.description: Water-logged scales reduce all incoming physical damage by 15 percent.
passiveAbility.name: Aquatic Armor

stats.name: Iguanodon
stats.funFact: The Iguanodon was one of the first three dinosaurs ever described scientifically and was originally thought to have a horn on its nose, which was actually a thumb spike.
stats.description: Iguanodon was a large, herbivorous dinosaur that flourished during the Early Cretaceous period. Known for its distinctively shaped thumb spikes, which likely served as defense against predators or tools for foraging, this ornithopod walked both on two legs and four. It possessed a unique dental structure, featuring rows of teeth perfect for grinding tough vegetation. Fossils discovered in Belgium provided significant insight into its herd behavior and skeletal anatomy. As a highly successful genus, Iguanodon represents a pivotal discovery in paleontology, shifting human understanding of prehistoric life and the ancient ecosystems of what is now Western Europe.

attacks.description: The Iguanodon focuses its mental energy to unleash a telekinetic strike from its thumb spike.
attacks.name: Psychic Spike

attacks.description: A powerful psychic surge that confuses the opponent and prevents them from attacking next turn.
attacks.name: Mind Over Matter

passiveAbility.description: Reduces damage taken from physical attacks by 15% due to a protective mental barrier.
passiveAbility.name: Psionic Shield

stats.name: Carnotaurus
stats.funFact: Its name means meat-eating bull due to the prominent horns above its eyes.
stats.description: Carnotaurus was a formidable theropod that patrolled the landscapes of ancient South America. It is best known for its unique appearance, featuring two sharp horns above its eyes and exceptionally small, vestigial arms that were even shorter than those of Tyrannosaurus rex. This predator was built for speed, boasting powerful, muscular hind limbs that allowed it to chase down agile prey across open plains. Despite its intimidating size, it possessed a relatively light skeleton, enabling rapid movement. Its specialized skull suggests it likely used its jaw like an axe to strike prey with high-speed precision during intense hunts.

attacks.description: A psychic charge flows through its horns upon impact.
Paralyzes the target with a mental vision of an apex predator.

stats.name: Dilophosaurus
stats.funFact: Despite its movie portrayal, there is no evidence that this dinosaur spat venom or had a neck frill.
stats.description: Dilophosaurus was a swift and agile predator from the Early Jurassic period, recognizable by the pair of distinctive crests atop its skull. These lightweight, blade-like crests were likely used for display rather than combat. As one of the earliest large theropods, it occupied a top predator niche, hunting with sharp, serrated teeth and powerful limbs. Its sleek build allowed for high-speed maneuvering, while its presence near water sources hints at an aquatic adaptation. Though often mischaracterized in pop culture, this dinosaur remains a fascinating example of early carnivorous evolution and specialized cranial anatomy during its historical timeframe.

attacks.name: Torrential Slash The user performs a swift strike infused with pressurized water to deal significant damage and stagger the opponent with a soaking effect. This attack highlights the creature's agility and mastery over aquatic forces during battle sequences. Damage: 45, Description: A rapid claw strike coated in a watery aura.

Crest Hydro-Burst Focusing its energy through its twin cranial ridges, the Dilophosaurus releases a concentrated blast of water. This ranged attack is devastating against earth-bound foes. Damage: 70, Description: A focused projectile of high-pressure water launched from the crests.

passiveAbility.name: Aqua Streamline Increases movement speed by 15 percent when health is below half, reflecting its fluid grace under pressure and allowing for tactical retreats or swift counter-strikes while navigating damp environments.

stats.name: Compsognathus
stats.funFact: Despite its small size, it had a highly efficient digestive system suited for a quick, predatory lifestyle.
stats.description: Compsognathus was a tiny, agile theropod dinosaur that lived during the Late Jurassic period. Often no larger than a common chicken, it was a swift hunter capable of snatching small lizards and insects with its sharp, serrated teeth. Its lightweight skeleton and elongated legs allowed it to dart through European landscapes with remarkable speed. Though physically small, its keen senses and tactical pack-hunting behaviors make it a formidable survivor. In this context, the species has developed a mysterious, latent psychic energy, allowing it to anticipate predator movements before they happen, making this diminutive carnivore a truly elusive and legendary prehistoric combatant.

stats.name: Gallimimus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to chicken mimic, reflecting its bird-like appearance and skeletal structure.
stats.description: Gallimimus was an agile, ostrich-like dinosaur that thrived during the Late Cretaceous period. With its slender build, long neck, and powerful, muscular legs, it was built for incredible speed, allowing it to evade larger predators effectively. Lacking teeth, this unique creature possessed a specialized beak used for grazing on plants and small insects alike. Its keen vision and lightning-fast reflexes made it a master of survival in the arid environments of Mongolia. As one of the largest ornithomimids ever discovered, the Gallimimus remains a fascinating example of evolutionary adaptation, bridging the gap between avian and reptilian movement patterns in prehistoric ecosystems.

attacks.name: Swift Sprint Kick. Damage: 35. Description: Uses its powerful legs to deliver a high-velocity strike while moving.
Beak Peck. Damage: 20. Description: A rapid, precision attack targeting the opponent's weak spots.

passiveAbility.name: Evasive Agility. Description: Increases dodge rate by 15% due to its natural speed and fluid movement patterns.

stats.name: Mosasaurus
stats.funFact: Despite being a dinosaur icon, the Mosasaurus was actually a giant marine lizard more closely related to modern snakes and monitor lizards than to dinosaurs.
stats.description: The Mosasaurus was the undisputed apex predator of the Late Cretaceous seas, dominating the oceans after the extinction of pliosaurs. Reaching lengths of up to 18 meters, it featured a massive, streamlined body equipped with powerful flippers and a long, deep tail capable of explosive bursts of speed. Its skull housed a secondary row of teeth on the roof of its mouth, ensuring that any prey caught in its jaws had no hope of escape. As a master of ambush, this terrifying marine reptile ruled the ancient waterways with unmatched ferocity, marking the peak of aquatic reptilian evolution before the K-Pg extinction.

attacks.description: Uses psychic energy to paralyze the target's motor functions before striking with bone-crushing jaws.
attacks.name: Mind-Crush Bite

attacks.description: Creates a localized temporal vortex that disorients the opponent, lowering their defenses for two turns.
attacks.name: Abyssal Distortion

passiveAbility.description: The Mosasaurus senses the psychic vibrations of enemies, increasing evasion against incoming physical attacks by 20%.
passiveAbility.name: Psychic Echo

stats.name: Pachycephalosaurus
stats.funFact: Its skull dome was reinforced with up to 25 centimeters of solid bone, likely used for intraspecies head-butting.
stats.description: Pachycephalosaurus is a unique genus of pachycephalosaurid dinosaurs known for its incredibly thick, domed skull. Thriving in the Late Cretaceous, these bipedal herbivores likely lived in herds across coastal plains and upland regions of North America. While its primary diet consisted of soft fruits and leafy vegetation, its defensive capabilities were formidable. The animal possessed a distinct array of spikes and bony knobs surrounding its head. Though debates persist regarding the function of its dome, most paleontologists agree it played a critical role in social rituals, allowing males to compete for dominance through powerful, high-impact combat displays.

attacks.description: The dinosaur charges forward, using its solid dome to strike with hydro-infused force.
attacks.name: Tidal Headbutt

attacks.description: A rapid spin that creates a mini-whirlpool to disorient the opponent.
attacks.name: Aqua Pivot

passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming physical damage by 10 percent due to the incredibly hardened bone structure.
passiveAbility.name: Reinforced Skull

stats.name: Baryonyx
stats.funFact: Baryonyx possessed an exceptionally large, hook-like claw on each thumb, which it likely used to spear fish from the water.
stats.description: Baryonyx is a fascinating spinosaurid theropod known for its specialized adaptations for an aquatic lifestyle. Unlike its more terrestrial cousins, this predator featured a long, narrow snout filled with conical teeth, perfectly designed for catching slippery fish in river systems. Its crocodile-like jaws and sturdy forelimbs allowed it to dominate riparian ecosystems during the Early Cretaceous. With a lean frame and powerful build, Baryonyx remains a testament to the diverse evolutionary strategies of predatory dinosaurs. Its discovery changed how paleontologists perceive the hunting habits of theropods, proving that these apex hunters were just as lethal in the water as on land.

attacks.description: The Baryonyx strikes with its massive thumb claw infused with electrical discharge.
attacks.name: Static Claw

attacks.description: A rapid series of bites that shocks the target upon contact.
attacks.name: Thunder Snap

passiveAbility.description: Increases attack speed by 15% when fighting in or near water sources.
passiveAbility.name: Aquatic Surge

stats.name: Deinonychus
stats.funFact: The name Deinonychus translates to terrible claw, referring to the large sickle-shaped talon on each of its hind feet.
stats.description: Deinonychus was a highly agile and intelligent predator that revolutionized our understanding of dinosaurs. Unlike the sluggish reptiles once depicted in early paleontological studies, this dromaeosaurid was bird-like, feathered, and swift. Its most iconic feature was the retractable, razor-sharp sickle claw on its second toe, which it likely used to pin down prey while delivering lethal strikes. Living in packs, these hunters coordinated attacks against dinosaurs significantly larger than themselves. Their discovery provided crucial evidence linking dinosaurs to modern-day birds, showcasing their sophisticated anatomy, keen senses, and dynamic hunting capabilities that dominated their ecosystems millions of years ago.

stats.name: Diplodocus
stats.funFact: The Diplodocus used its whip-like tail as a defensive weapon and potentially for communication by creating loud sonic booms.
stats.description: Diplodocus is one of the most easily identifiable dinosaurs, characterized by its incredibly long neck and whip-like tail. As a massive sauropod, it roamed the floodplains of North America during the Late Jurassic period, browsing on high-reaching vegetation with its peg-like teeth. Its immense body size was supported by four sturdy, pillar-like legs, allowing it to navigate diverse terrain. While often perceived as a gentle giant, its tail served as a powerful deterrent against predators. Despite its gigantic stature, its brain was relatively small, showcasing the remarkable biological efficiency required to sustain such a prehistoric behemoth in its environment.

attacks.description: Swings its massive tail through the water to create a tidal wave impact.
attacks.name: Tidal Tail Whip

attacks.description: Absorbs aquatic energy to unleash a powerful blast from its throat.
attacks.name: Hydro Torrent

passiveAbility.description: Increases damage resistance when positioned in watery or swampy terrain.
passiveAbility.name: Amphibious Resilience

stats.name: Giganotosaurus
stats.funFact: Its skull was nearly six feet long, and its teeth were serrated like steak knives for slicing through thick hide.
stats.description: The Giganotosaurus was one of the largest terrestrial carnivores to ever roam the Earth. Dominating the landscape of Cretaceous South America, this apex predator grew to massive sizes, potentially rivaling or even exceeding the T-Rex in length. Equipped with a powerful jaw, sharp serrated teeth, and long muscular legs, it was a lethal hunter capable of bringing down the largest sauropods of its time. With its intimidating presence and immense power, the Giganotosaurus stands as a titan of history, embodying the peak of predatory evolution during the final age of the dinosaurs before their ultimate extinction from our planet.

attacks.description: A bone-crushing strike that ignores target armor.
attacks.name: Savage Shred

stats.name: Plateosaurus
stats.funFact: Plateosaurus was one of the first dinosaurs to be scientifically described and is often called the 'flat lizard' because of its elongated body shape.
stats.description: Plateosaurus was a dominant bipedal herbivore during the Late Triassic period, acting as a direct ancestor to the massive sauropods that would later rule the Jurassic. It possessed a slender frame, a long flexible neck for reaching high foliage, and sharp thumb claws used for defense or foraging. As an early prosauropod, it roamed in massive herds across Europe, utilizing its agility to escape predators. Its unique water-attuned physiology allows it to thrive in marshy environments, making it a surprisingly elusive dinosaur that can traverse aquatic terrain with ease, showcasing remarkable adaptation for an ancient land-dwelling giant.

attacks.description: The Plateosaurus swings its powerful tail through a water current to knock back enemies.
attacks.name: Hydro-Tail Swipe

attacks.description: It stomps onto a puddle, creating a surging wave that engulfs the opponent in cold water.
attacks.name: Tidal Stomp

passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming damage by 10% when fighting in watery or swampy locations.
passiveAbility.name: Aquatic Grace

stats.name: Archaeopteryx
stats.funFact: Often called the first bird, it possessed teeth and a long bony tail unlike modern avian species.
stats.description: Archaeopteryx serves as a vital transitional fossil between non-avian feathered dinosaurs and modern birds. Living approximately 150 million years ago, this creature featured a unique combination of reptilian traits, such as jaws with sharp teeth and a long tail, alongside bird-like characteristics like wings and feathers. While likely a weak flyer, its discovery in the Solnhofen limestone provided crucial evidence for evolutionary biology. Archaeopteryx highlights the blurred lines between ancient dinosaurs and today's aerial inhabitants, standing as a pivotal evolutionary link that redefined our understanding of how flight and avian anatomy first emerged within the dinosaur lineage.

stats.name: Kentrosaurus
stats.funFact: Unlike its famous cousin Stegosaurus, Kentrosaurus had a pair of long, sharp spikes extending from its shoulders for defense.
stats.description: Kentrosaurus is a fascinating member of the stegosaurian family known for its unique defensive armor. Featuring a double row of small plates along its neck and back that transition into lethal spikes toward the tail, it was well-equipped to deter predators. Its low-slung, herbivorous build allowed it to graze on ground-level foliage efficiently. While relatively smaller than other stegosaurids, its specialized weaponry made it a formidable opponent in the prehistoric wild. This dinosaur demonstrates the evolutionary ingenuity of Jurassic herbivores adapting to survive alongside some of the world's most aggressive and iconic apex carnivores during their prime.

attacks.description: Swings its spiked tail with psychic energy to inflict physical damage and stun the opponent.
attacks.name: Psionic Tail Lash

attacks.description: Emits a mental pulse that confuses the target and reduces their defensive capabilities.
attacks.name: Mind Spike

passiveAbility.description: Reflects 10% of physical damage back onto the attacker when struck by a melee move.
passiveAbility.name: Psychic Spikes

stats.name: Maiasaura
stats.funFact: Its name translates to Good Mother Lizard because it was one of the first dinosaurs found to show evidence of communal nesting and caring for its young.
stats.description: Maiasaura was a large, duck-billed hadrosaur that lived in large herds during the Late Cretaceous period. Characterized by its flat skull and small crest, this herbivore roamed the floodplains of what is now Montana. The discovery of extensive nesting grounds provided groundbreaking evidence that some dinosaurs were social creatures that nurtured their offspring in rookeries. As a Psychic-type entity in this context, the Maiasaura utilizes its heightened emotional awareness and maternal instinct to influence the battlefield, projecting calm or confusion onto opponents while protecting its allies from harm with an unseen, powerful mental barrier.

attacks.description: The user emits a calming pulse that reduces the opponent's aggressive energy.
attacks.name: Maternal Serenity

attacks.description: A psychic shockwave is released, reflecting the user's protective intensity.
attacks.name: Protective Echo

passiveAbility.description: Allies on the field recover 10 HP at the end of every turn due to the soothing aura.
passiveAbility.name: Nurturing Aura

stats.name: Oviraptor
stats.funFact: Its name means egg thief, but it was likely just protecting its own nest.
stats.description: Oviraptor was a bird-like, feathered dinosaur that lived in the arid environments of what is now Mongolia. Despite its misleading name, paleontological evidence suggests that these creatures were actually attentive parents, brooding over their own eggs rather than stealing them. With its short, deep skull and toothless beak, it likely crushed eggs, seeds, and small prey for sustenance. Its agile, bipedal form and sharp, curved claws made it a highly efficient scavenger in its ecosystem. Its unique crest on its head was likely used for display purposes, signaling status or attracting potential mates in their social groups.

attacks.description: Delivers a rapid strike that shocks the opponent with static electricity.
Uses its sharp talons to conduct a high-voltage surge upon impact.

stats.name: Styracosaurus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to spiked lizard because of the unique arrangement of horns protruding from its frill.
stats.description: Styracosaurus was a striking herbivorous dinosaur characterized by a massive frill adorned with long, sharp spikes and a prominent nasal horn. Living in herds during the Late Cretaceous, this sturdy ceratopsian used its intimidating appearance and formidable armor to deter apex predators of the era. Its powerful beak allowed it to efficiently graze on tough ferns and cycads. Despite its aggressive aesthetic, it was a calm dweller of the coastal plains, relying on social structure and structural defenses to thrive in an environment shared with massive tyrannosaurs. It remains a fascinating icon of prehistoric defensive evolution.

attacks.name: Static Spike Thruster: Charges the nasal horn with high voltage to puncture through enemy armor while delivering a paralyzing electric shock upon impact. Damage: 45
Frill Flare Discharge: Emits a blinding arc of electricity from the decorative frill, stunning all opponents in a wide radius and clearing the field. Damage: 70

passiveAbility.name: Conductive Defense: Any physical contact against the Styracosaurus results in a minor electrical recoil that damages the attacker over time.

stats.name: Torosaurus
stats.funFact: Torosaurus possessed one of the largest skulls of any land animal, with its frill reaching up to 2.6 meters in length.
stats.description: Torosaurus was a massive ceratopsid dinosaur characterized by its immense, fenestrated skull and thick neck frill. Often compared to its relative Triceratops, this dinosaur occupied the lush ecosystems of the North American plains. Its massive horns provided formidable defense against apex predators like Tyrannosaurus. The unique gaps in its frill, known as fenestrae, likely helped reduce the weight of its enormous head while allowing for significant muscle attachment. As an herbivore, it grazed extensively on tough, low-lying vegetation. Its impressive resilience and sheer structural mass make it a legendary tank within the prehistoric animal kingdom.

attacks.description: The Torosaurus channels electrical energy into its massive brow horns for a devastating electrified charge.
attacks.name: Thunder Horn Stab

attacks.description: It slams its heavy tail into the ground, sending a high-voltage shockwave through the terrain to stun opponents.
attacks.name: Static Ground Pound

passiveAbility.description: The dinosaur generates a static field that reflects 10% of physical damage back onto the attacker.
passiveAbility.name: Conductive Hide

stats.name: Suchomimus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to Crocodile Mimic because of its long, narrow snout filled with interlocking teeth designed for catching slippery fish.
stats.description: Suchomimus is a remarkable spinosaurid theropod that roamed the river systems of Northern Africa during the Early Cretaceous period. Unlike most large predators of its time, this dinosaur was highly specialized for a semi-aquatic lifestyle. Its elongated, crocodilian-like skull and conical teeth allowed it to excel at hunting prey in shallow waters. With massive forelimbs equipped with large, hooked claws, Suchomimus could easily snatch aquatic prey or defend itself against terrestrial rivals. This unique evolutionary adaptation makes it a fascinating specimen that highlights the incredible diversity of predatory strategies found throughout dinosaur history in the ancient Sahara region.

stats.name: Acrocanthosaurus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to 'high-spined lizard' due to the tall neural spines along its vertebrae that likely supported a massive hump or ridge of muscle.
stats.description: Acrocanthosaurus was a formidable apex predator that dominated the North American landscape long before the reign of the Tyrannosaurus rex. This massive theropod possessed powerful jaws and serrated teeth designed to tear through the thick hides of sauropods. Its distinctive spinal ridge suggests a highly complex biology, potentially used for communication or heat regulation. With a balanced frame and lethal precision, this psychic-aligned titan uses its immense mental focus to anticipate prey movements across vast territories. As a apex hunter of the Early Cretaceous, it remains one of the most enigmatic and physically imposing dinosaurs ever discovered by paleontologists.

Its build was lean yet incredibly powerful, allowing it to move with surprising agility for its size. As an ambush predator, it dominated its ecosystem, leaving little room for competitors. Its anatomy suggests it relied heavily on its keen sense of smell to locate food. Despite its heavy build, it was a highly efficient hunter, dominating its ecosystem for millions of years. Despite its relatively small stature compared to other titanosaurs, its robust build and protective armor made it a resilient species. Its discovery provided paleontologists with groundbreaking evidence that even massive, plant-eating sauropods developed sophisticated defensive mechanisms to survive in a world filled with dangerous, sharp-toothed predators.

stats.name: Amargasaurus

Its low-slung, peg-like teeth suggest it was a selective browser, feeding on low-lying vegetation. This herbivorous creature stands out in the fossil record for its bizarre anatomy, providing paleontologists with vital insights into the evolutionary diversity of the famous sauropod family.

With its low center of gravity and sturdy frame, it was essentially a living fortress, browsing on low-growing vegetation while remaining impervious to most threats of the Late Cretaceous period.

Its anatomy reflects a specialized adaptation to a localized environment, and its fossilized remains have provided scientists with unprecedented insight into the soft tissue characteristics of armored dinosaurs, marking it as a quintessential specimen of the Australian Cretaceous fossil record.

stats.name: Lambeosaurus

Though it was a peaceful plant-eater, its massive size and muscular build allowed it to withstand challenges from predators. Its serrated teeth and specialized, flexible jaw provided a distinct advantage, ensuring it could hold onto struggling prey. Its crocodile-like skull and hooked claws allowed it to secure slippery catches with ease. Though once obscured by scientific tampering, modern research has cemented its place as a unique evolutionary bridge, showcasing how early spinosaurs mastered diverse aquatic ecosystems while maintaining an imposing presence on solid land.

stats.name: Kulindadromeus

stats.description: Kulindadromeus is a revolutionary discovery in paleontology, fundamentally altering our understanding of dinosaur evolution. Its most striking feature is the presence of diverse integumentary structures, including simple filaments and complex, compound feathers, suggesting that feathers were common among early dinosaurs, not just theropods. Its body was specialized for speed, featuring a slender build and long hind legs adapted for navigating the lush, prehistoric landscapes of Siberia. Despite its diminutive size, it holds a massive place in scientific history, providing a crucial bridge in the evolutionary timeline of feathered dinosaurs.

stats.name: Majungasaurus
stats.funFact: Evidence suggests that Majungasaurus practiced cannibalism, as its fossilized bones show teeth marks matching its own species.

Though its arms were exceptionally small, its powerful jaws and serrated teeth made it the apex predator of its ecosystem. Recent research into its remains has shed light on complex social behaviors and internal anatomy, solidifying its place as one of the most fascinating carnivores of the Late Cretaceous period in the Southern Hemisphere.

stats.name: Microraptor
stats.funFact: Microraptor is one of the few known dinosaurs to possess flight feathers on both its forelimbs and hindlimbs, creating a four-winged gliding profile.
stats.description: Microraptor was a tiny, agile dromaeosaurid dinosaur that lived roughly 120 million years ago. Despite its diminutive size, it was an expert aerial hunter within the lush forests of Early Cretaceous China. Its unique anatomy features long, asymmetrical flight feathers on its wings and legs, suggesting that it was capable of gliding between tree branches to ambush small prey like lizards and mammals. This remarkable creature provides vital clues into the evolutionary transition between non-avian dinosaurs and modern birds, showcasing the complex ways early paravians experimented with flight-like behaviors and structural adaptations for survival.

attacks.name: Aerial Ambush Dive bomb attack from above dealing rapid damage to foes while keeping safe from reach issues using claws and teeth to shred armor at high speed during the drop.
Wing Blade Slash Utilizing stiffened feathers and sharp claws to deliver quick lacerations that disrupt enemy concentration during high-speed combat maneuvers across the battlefield territory.

stats.name: Nodosaurus
stats.funFact: Nodosaurus translates to 'knobbed lizard' because of the bony, armor-plated nodules embedded in its skin.
stats.description: Nodosaurus was a formidable ankylosaurian dinosaur known for its heavy, protective armor consisting of oval bony plates called osteoderms. Unlike its later relatives, it lacked a heavy tail club, relying instead on its thick hide and low profile to deflect predators. This quadrupedal herbivore roamed the coastal plains of North America, using its narrow beak to selectively graze on low-lying vegetation. Despite its slow movement, its robust physical construction made it an incredibly difficult target for large theropods. Its existence during the Late Cretaceous highlights the evolutionary arms race between armored prey and the predators that stalked them.

attacks.description: The Nodosaurus forcefully rams its armored side into the opponent, causing internal impact damage.
attacks.name: Heavy Ram

attacks.description: The dinosaur lowers its head and swings its spiked, armor-covered neck to deter aggressive attackers.
attacks.name: Armor Bash

passiveAbility.description: Reduces damage taken from physical attacks by 20% due to its dense, thick osteoderm shielding.
passiveAbility.name: Knobbed Plating

stats.name: Ornithomimus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to bird mimic, reflecting its striking physical resemblance to modern ostriches.
stats.description: Ornithomimus was a swift, bipedal dinosaur that thrived in the lush floodplains of Late Cretaceous North America. Featuring a small, toothless beak and large, alert eyes, it possessed an elegant, bird-like skeletal structure optimized for incredible speed. While primarily feeding on plants, insects, and small vertebrates, it relied heavily on its agility to escape larger predators. Today, it remains a key example of convergent evolution. With its Psychic energy, this specimen exhibits heightened sensory awareness, allowing it to anticipate threats long before they appear, making it an elusive and masterful survivor in any high-stakes prehistoric battle encounter.

attacks.description: The dinosaur delivers a rapid flurry of pecks while psychically predicting the enemy's next dodge.
attacks.name: Mind-Read Peck

attacks.description: Channels psychic energy into a focused burst of speed, striking the opponent with a psychic kinetic wave.
attacks.name: Psionic Dash

passiveAbility.description: Increases evasion chance by 20% due to the dinosaur's ability to sense incoming attacks through precognition.
passiveAbility.name: Sixth Sense

stats.name: Protoceratops
stats.funFact: Protoceratops is often cited as the potential inspiration for the mythical griffin due to its beak and large frill.
stats.description: Protoceratops was a sheep-sized ceratopsian dinosaur that thrived in the arid environments of what is now Mongolia. Unlike its later, larger cousins like Triceratops, it lacked prominent horns, instead sporting a massive neck frill that likely served for display purposes or social signaling. Fossil discoveries have provided incredible insights into its behavior, including rare instances of them being preserved alongside predators. With a powerful beak and a robust, quadrupedal build, this clever herbivore was well-adapted to scavenging low-lying vegetation in a harsh desert ecosystem, proving that small dinosaurs could be incredibly resilient and evolutionarily successful.

attacks.name: Static Beak Strikeetail Snap-Backer, damage: 35, description: A quick, shocking jab with its hardened beak that delivers a mild electric charge.
Frill Surge, damage: 60, description: The dinosaur flares its frill to discharge stored lightning energy, stunning nearby opponents for one turn.

passiveAbility.name: Desert Conductivity, description: Gains 10 bonus HP when fighting in sandy or arid terrain types.

stats.name: Rajasaurus
stats.funFact: Its unique head crest suggests it was used for display or as a means of species recognition among its kin.
stats.description: Rajasaurus was a formidable abelisaurid theropod that roamed the Narmada Valley in India approximately 67 million years ago. Known for its distinctively thickened, crested skull, this apex predator sat at the top of the local food chain. Its powerful hind limbs suggest it was an efficient hunter, capable of taking down large sauropods that shared its environment. Despite being discovered relatively recently, fossils have provided paleontologists with significant insights into the biodiversity of the Indo-Madagascan region during the Late Cretaceous, highlighting the evolutionary distinctiveness of Indian dinosaurs before the dramatic environmental shifts of the era.

attacks.name: Static Bite: 45 damage, paralyzes the target for one turn with electrical discharge.
Crest Crash: 60 damage, slams the head into the opponent with a surge of kinetic lightning energy.

passiveAbility.name: Storm Aura: Increases damage output by 10 percent during rainy or stormy conditions.

stats.name: Saurolophus
stats.funFact: Saurolophus possessed a unique solid, spike-like crest on its head that was likely used for vocal signaling between herd members.
stats.description: Saurolophus was a large hadrosaurid, or duck-billed dinosaur, known for its distinct cranial anatomy. It flourished during the Maastrichtian stage of the Late Cretaceous, roaming environments that ranged from coastal plains to inland floodplains. Its body was built for both quadrupedal grazing and occasional bipedal movement. The hallmark of this dinosaur is the hollow-based spike extending from the back of the skull, which may have supported a fleshy display structure. As a social creature, Saurolophus traveled in herds to graze on tough vegetation, using its sophisticated dental batteries to grind down food effectively while avoiding numerous apex predators.

attacks.name: Static Crest Pulse, damage: 45, description: Sends a jolt of bio-electric energy through its head crest to stun opponents.
Thunderous Stomp, damage: 60, description: A heavy impact attack that sends shockwaves through the ground, dealing electrified physical damage.

passiveAbility.name: Herd Voltage Conductance, description: Gains 10 HP regeneration when standing near allied dinosaur cards on the field.

stats.name: Sinoceratops
stats.funFact: It is the first ceratopsid dinosaur ever discovered in China, proving these dinosaurs lived in Asia as well as North America.
stats.description: Sinoceratops was a formidable herbivore from the Late Cretaceous period, distinguished by its unique, fan-shaped frill adorned with curved spikes. Unlike its cousins, it possessed a single, blunt nasal horn and lacked brow horns, giving it a distinctive silhouette. This dinosaur thrived in the lush, river-laden landscapes of what is now modern-day Shandong Province. Primarily a browser, it utilized its sturdy beak to process tough vegetation. Its discovery was a landmark paleontological event, significantly expanding our understanding of the geographical distribution and evolutionary diversification of ceratopsian dinosaurs, highlighting a complex and varied history across the ancient eastern continents.

attacks.description: The dinosaur charges forward, using its massive frill to bash opponents with significant force.
attacks.name: Frill Bash

attacks.description: A swift strike from the blunt nasal horn that disrupts the opponent's balance and defense.
attacks.name: Nasal Thrust

passiveAbility.description: The thick, spiked frill provides an extra layer of defense, reducing incoming physical damage by 10%.
passiveAbility.name: Natural Armor

stats.name: Therizinosaurus
stats.funFact: It possessed the longest claws of any known animal, reaching up to one meter in length, which it likely used for foliage gathering.
stats.description: Therizinosaurus is one of the most unique dinosaurs to ever walk the earth. Despite belonging to the theropod group, which primarily consists of meat-eaters, this massive creature evolved to be a gentle giant. With a long neck and a pot-bellied frame, it spent its days browsing through lush forests. Its most striking features are its gargantuan, scythe-like claws that provide a formidable defense against predators. In this card set, Therizinosaurus harnesses the power of molten magma, channeling intense Fire energy through its razor-sharp claws to incinerate any threat foolish enough to challenge its peaceful territorial browsing.

attacks.description: Swipes with blazing claws that ignite the target on impact.
attacks.name: Scorched Scythe

attacks.description: Stomps the ground, causing a localized volcanic eruption.
attacks.name: Magma Tremor

passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming physical damage by 20% due to its thick, fire-resistant hide.
passiveAbility.name: Thermal Hide

stats.name: Troodon
stats.funFact: Troodon is widely considered one of the most intelligent non-avian dinosaurs due to its unusually large brain-to-body ratio.
stats.description: Troodon was a small, agile, and bird-like dinosaur that roamed the forests of North America during the late Cretaceous period. Known for its keen eyesight and acute hearing, it was a highly efficient nocturnal predator. Its slender build, gripping hands, and large, sickle-shaped claws on its feet allowed it to capture small mammals and lizards while also foraging for plant matter. Its sophisticated brain structure suggests complex social behaviors and sharp problem-solving skills, making it a formidable survivor. Despite its diminutive stature, Troodon stands out in the fossil record as a pinnacle of dinosaurian evolution and cognitive advancement.

stats.name: Utahraptor
stats.funFact: Utahraptors are significantly larger than the Velociraptors popularized by films, reaching sizes comparable to a grizzly bear.
stats.description: Utahraptor was a formidable dromaeosaurid predator that dominated the Early Cretaceous landscape of North America. Distinguished by its massive, sickle-shaped toe claws and powerful build, this dinosaur was an apex predator capable of bringing down prey much larger than itself. Unlike its smaller relatives, the Utahraptor relied on sheer physical strength and coordinated pack tactics to subdue victims. Its bird-like appearance, including likely plumage, suggests a highly active and intelligent hunter. Paleontological evidence indicates that it filled a unique ecological niche, maintaining balance within its diverse ecosystem through relentless pursuit and lethal, precise strikes against contemporary herbivores.

stats.name: Zuniceratops
stats.funFact: It is the earliest known ceratopsian dinosaur to possess brow horns, serving as the evolutionary precursor to later, larger horned dinosaurs like Triceratops.
stats.description: Zuniceratops is a fascinating genus of ceratopsian dinosaur that inhabited North America approximately 90 million years ago. As one of the earliest members of its lineage to exhibit prominent brow horns, it provides crucial insight into the development of cranial ornaments in herbivorous dinosaurs. Its relatively small stature and agile build suggest it was a social animal, likely living in groups to deter predators within its dense forest habitats. With a distinctive frill and sharp beak, Zuniceratops represents a vital link in the evolutionary chain that would eventually lead to the iconic giants of the late Cretaceous period.

attacks.name: Static Charge Horns Scrape Damage: 45. Description: The horns glow with electric current causing additional shock damage to enemies. Damage: 45
Thundering Stomp. Description: A heavy charge that creates a small localized lightning storm around the target. Damage: 65

passiveAbility.name: Conductive Crest. Description: When struck by physical attacks, the Zuniceratops releases a static discharge that stuns the attacker for one turn.

stats.name: Dicraeosaurus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to 'forked lizard' due to the distinctive Y-shaped neural spines along its neck and back.
stats.description: Dicraeosaurus was a unique sauropod characterized by its remarkably short neck compared to other long-necked dinosaurs of the Jurassic period. Living in what is now Tanzania, this creature moved in herds through lush floodplains, utilizing its specialized teeth to graze on low-lying vegetation. Unlike its massive diplodocid cousins, it possessed a smaller, lighter frame with iconic bifid neural spines that likely supported large, horn-like projections or sail structures. Its defensive strategy relied on social awareness and its robust whip-like tail, which provided a dangerous deterrent against the opportunistic predators that stalked the ancient landscapes of East Africa.

stats.name: Eoraptor
stats.funFact: Eoraptor is one of the earliest known dinosaurs, dating back roughly 231 million years to the Ischigualasto Formation.
stats.description: Eoraptor is a small, agile dinosaur that lived during the dawn of the dinosaur age. Measuring about one meter in length, this bipedal predator occupied a transitional ecological niche, consuming a varied diet of small animals and vegetation. Its lightweight frame allowed for impressive bursts of speed to evade larger predators. Paleontologists consider it a crucial link in evolutionary history, helping scientists understand the ancestral traits that would eventually lead to the massive theropods of the later Jurassic periods. With its sharp teeth and nimble claws, Eoraptor represents the humble beginnings of a dynasty that dominated Earth for millions of years.
