attacks.description: A swift strike from the hind claws, specifically designed to bypass enemy shields and deal consistent damage. This attack is efficient and relies on the Eoraptor's speed to deliver a quick blow before retreating to a safe distance, making it difficult for opponents to track its position during intense melee exchanges.
attacks.name: Claw Strike

passiveAbility.description: Eoraptor gains +10 speed and evasion while its health is below 50%, representing its desperate and nimble nature when under pressure on the field.
passiveAbility.name: Adrenaline Rush

stats.name: Futabasaurus
stats.funFact: It is the first elasmosaurid plesiosaur discovered in Japan, named after the Futaba Group where its remains were found.
stats.description: Futabasaurus was a formidable marine reptile, specifically an elasmosaurid plesiosaur characterized by its exceptionally long neck and small head. Thriving in the shallow seas of the Late Cretaceous, it likely navigated the ancient waters of what is now Japan. Its body was streamlined, equipped with four powerful flippers that allowed it to swim with great agility. While its diet consisted primarily of small fish and cephalopods, it was an apex predator of its localized environment. Paleontologists identify the species by its unique neck vertebrae and skull structure, which distinguish it from other plesiosaur specimens found across the globe.

stats.name: Gondwanatitan
stats.funFact: This dinosaur is named in honor of the Brazilian paleontologist Fausto Luiz de Souza Cunha.
stats.description: Gondwanatitan was a massive titanosaurian sauropod that roamed the landmass of South America during the Late Cretaceous epoch. Characterized by its long neck, thick tail, and pillarlike limbs, this herbivore was designed to browse high-canopy vegetation inaccessible to smaller creatures. Its discovery provided critical insights into the diversity of titanosaurs in the Southern Hemisphere, specifically within the Bauru Group in Brazil. Despite its immense size, Gondwanatitan was a relatively compact sauropod compared to its giant relatives. Its existence highlights the complex ecosystem dynamics and evolutionary success of specialized long-necked dinosaurs that dominated the terrestrial habitats millions of years before the K-Pg extinction event.

stats.name: Guanlong
stats.funFact: Its name translates to Crown Dragon due to the elaborate, hollow crest found atop its skull, likely used for display purposes.
stats.description: Guanlong is a primitive member of the tyrannosauroid superfamily, serving as a key evolutionary ancestor to the mighty Tyrannosaurus rex. Despite its smaller stature compared to its more famous descendants, it possessed sharp teeth and agile limbs that made it an efficient predator. Found in the Shishugou Formation, it thrived in the lush environments of ancient China. The presence of a prominent, delicate crest suggests that social signaling and mating displays were already sophisticated behaviors in these early predators. Its discovery provides crucial insights into the ancestral skeletal structure and diversification of the iconic tyrannosaur lineage across prehistoric time.

attacks.description: The user bites the opponent with molten, superheated jaws that inflict fire damage.
attacks.name: Ember Bite

attacks.description: The user exhales a concentrated blast of volcanic heat from its crest, scorching the battlefield.
attacks.name: Crest Inferno

passiveAbility.description: Increases damage of all fire-type attacks when the user's health falls below 50 percent.
passiveAbility.name: Smoldering Fury

stats.name: Hypsilophodon
stats.funFact: Once thought to be a tree-dweller due to its grasping toes, it is now known to be a fast-running ground dweller.
stats.description: Hypsilophodon was a small, bipedal ornithopod dinosaur that thrived in the lush environments of the Early Cretaceous period. Known for its agile frame and specialized beak, it was expertly adapted for grazing on low-lying vegetation. Despite its diminutive size compared to larger predators, Hypsilophodon possessed impressive speed and sharp eyesight, allowing it to navigate complex habitats with ease. Its lightweight skeleton and stiffened tail provided excellent balance while maneuvering at high velocities. Today, this dinosaur serves as a crucial link in understanding the evolution of small, fast-moving herbivores that eventually diversified into the wide array of ornithopods seen later.

attacks.description: The Hypsilophodon uses its powerful hind legs to deliver a rapid, precise kick to the opponent.
attacks.name: Swift Kick

attacks.description: The dinosaur charges head-on with surprising momentum to bash into the target.
attacks.name: Cretaceous Charge

passiveAbility.description: Increases evasion rate by 15% due to its incredibly agile and erratic running pattern.
passiveAbility.name: Agile Dodger

stats.name: Lexovisaurus
stats.funFact: Lexovisaurus was the first stegosaurid dinosaur to be formally named from European remains, though initially misidentified as Omosaurus.
stats.description: Lexovisaurus was a distinct stegosaurid herbivore roaming the landscapes of Middle Jurassic Europe. Characterized by its unique armor, this dinosaur featured both tall, thin dorsal plates and elongated spikes along its shoulders, which likely served as both defense mechanisms and displays for potential rivals or mates. With a low-slung body and a beak adapted for stripping vegetation, it thrived in dense forest clearings. Its evolutionary lineage remains a subject of intense paleontological debate, serving as a critical link in understanding how early armored dinosaurs diversified and migrated across the shifting continents before the rise of the later, iconic stegosaurids.

attacks.description: Uses psychic energy to confuse the opponent, making attacks miss 20% of the time.
attacks.name: Psionic Spike

attacks.description: Unleashes a powerful mental blast that pierces through physical armor.
attacks.name: Mind Shell Cracker

passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming physical damage by 10% as the dinosaur creates a shimmering psychic barrier.
passiveAbility.name: Mental Aegis

stats.name: Linhenykus
stats.funFact: Linhenykus is the only known dinosaur that possessed a single functional finger on each hand.
stats.description: Linhenykus is a remarkably small theropod belonging to the Alvarezsauridae family. Despite its tiny stature, it represents a significant evolutionary milestone due to its unique skeletal structure. Unlike its relatives that had reduced digits, this specimen uniquely featured only one single functional clawed finger on its forelimbs. Scientists believe this digit was specialized for digging into insect mounds to reach termites or larvae. Its bird-like appearance and long, slender legs suggest it was a fast-moving, agile predator that navigated the arid environments of the Gobi Desert with incredible precision, avoiding larger Cretaceous predators through speed and stealthy behavior.

attacks.name: Single Claw Slashing Strike in rapid succession against weak armor plates of enemies and insects alike, dealing moderate damage while increasing evasion chance for the next turn for this agile and quick dinosaur creature, effectively punishing heavier and slower opponents through constant movement and precise strikes at vulnerable points of armor or skin, proving its high combat efficiency even despite its very small physical presence during intense prehistoric territorial battles and survival scenarios in the harsh desert environment it inhabited millions of years ago in what is now modern China's vast and unforgiving landscape. (Damage: 25, Description: A precise strike with its singular claw aimed at vital weaknesses.)

Desert Scramble, causing quick confusion to the opponent while dealing heavy impact damage from a sudden, low-profile charge move that utilizes its high speed to bypass defenses, making it a nightmare for larger opponents who struggle to track its rapid, erratic movement patterns throughout the heat of battle. (Damage: 40, Description: A swift, lunging charge that disrupts enemy focus.)

passiveAbility.name: Single-Digit Focus, which grants a high critical hit ratio because its concentrated power is channeled entirely into its singular digit for maximum force per strike against any enemy armor or hide during intense combat situations, effectively allowing it to pierce through defenses that would normally stop larger predators in their tracks, highlighting the evolutionary advantage of its highly specialized anatomy compared to multi-fingered relatives that distribute force differently. (Description: Increases critical hit chance by 15% during every turn of battle.)

stats.name: Monoclonius
stats.funFact: Its name means 'single sprout' because its teeth appear to grow in a single row rather than replacing in multiple generations like other ceratopsians.
stats.description: Monoclonius is a mid-sized ceratopsian dinosaur recognized by its prominent single nasal horn and a wide, shield-like bony frill protecting its neck. Primarily grazing on low-lying vegetation, this herbivore roamed the swampy coastal plains of Western North America alongside larger relatives like Centrosaurus. While often overshadowed by the iconic Triceratops, Monoclonius played a crucial role in understanding the evolution of horned dinosaurs. Its sturdy build and aggressive defense mechanisms allowed it to survive in environments teeming with apex predators, utilizing its sharp beak and massive skull to ward off threats in its ancient, lush habitat.

attacks.name: Magma Charge Horns Incinerate surroundings with a fiery impact that deals significant piercing damage to enemy defenses while leaving a scorching heat effect on the battlefield. Damage: 65, Description: A fiery charge attack dealing high impact damage.

Embershield Pulse Radiates a wave of intense heat from the frill that damages nearby attackers and boosts the user's defensive capabilities against elemental assaults. Damage: 40, Description: A defensive wave of fire energy.

passiveAbility.name: Thermal Resistance Body heat is contained within the frill, allowing this dinosaur to withstand extreme temperature fluctuations during combat, reducing incoming Fire-type damage by 20%. Description: Provides resistance against heat-based attacks.

stats.name: Nemegtosaurus
stats.funFact: Its skull was surprisingly narrow, leading paleontologists to believe it had a very specific, selective grazing habit.
stats.description: Nemegtosaurus was a long-necked titanosaurian sauropod that roamed the Gobi Desert roughly 70 million years ago. Despite its massive body, it is primarily known for its remarkably elongated, horse-like skull which contains peg-like teeth designed for stripping leaves. Unlike most sauropods whose skeletons are found in abundance, Nemegtosaurus remains are incredibly rare, making it a mysterious subject of study. Its anatomy suggests it was well-adapted to the semi-arid environments of the Nemegt Formation. As a heavy herbivore, it likely moved in small herds to protect against predators, using its massive tail as a formidable defensive tool against encroaching threats.

attacks.description: Swings its massive, muscular tail to crush the opponent.
Uses its specialized teeth to drain the opponent's vitality.

stats.name: Opisthocoelicaudia
stats.funFact: Its name translates to 'posterior hollow tail vertebrae' due to the unique shape of its tail bones.
stats.description: Opisthocoelicaudia was a robust sauropod that roamed the arid landscapes of the Late Cretaceous. Unlike its long-necked relatives, it possessed a stout, heavy body structure that provided immense stability. Its uniquely shaped tail vertebrae suggest it may have been able to support its weight on its hind legs for brief periods, perhaps to graze on higher vegetation. As a master of the Gobi Desert, this creature utilized its size as a natural fortress against predators. Today, it remains a fascination for paleontologists, serving as a vital link in understanding the diversity and evolution of Late Cretaceous titanosaurs in Asia.

attacks.name: Thunder Stomp: A crushing strike that discharges stored lightning into the ground to shock surrounding enemies for 45 damage.
Static Tail Whip: A swift, electrified sweep of its heavy tail dealing 60 damage and paralyzing foes.

passiveAbility.name: Grounded Charge: Absorbs surrounding electrical energy, increasing defense by 15% when attacked by Lightning-type moves.

stats.name: Ornitholestes
stats.funFact: Its name means 'bird robber' because it was originally thought to hunt primitive birds, though it likely preferred small lizards.
stats.description: Ornitholestes was a nimble and agile theropod that lived during the Late Jurassic period in what is now Wyoming. With a slender, long-tailed frame and sharp, grasping claws, it was perfectly adapted for hunting small vertebrate prey in the dense forest undergrowth. Its distinct nasal crest suggests it may have used sound or display to communicate with others of its kind. As a swift predator, it utilized its keen senses and light body weight to ambush unsuspecting creatures. Today, it remains a vital subject for paleontologists studying the evolutionary link between small theropods and the earliest avian species.

attacks.name: Tidal Scratch115, description: A swift, water-infused claw strike that leaves the opponent drenched and vulnerable, damage: 35
Current Agility, description: Utilizes a surrounding water barrier to increase evasion while delivering a quick bite to the throat, damage: 20}
Aquatic Pounce, damage: 45, description: Leaps from hidden water sources to deliver a powerful, soaking blow.

stats.name: Patagosaurus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to Patagonian lizard, referring to the region in Argentina where its fossils were first unearthed in the late 1970s.
stats.description: Patagosaurus was a robust cetiosaurid sauropod that roamed the lush landscapes of South America during the Middle Jurassic period. As a mid-sized sauropod, it possessed a long neck for reaching foliage and a heavy, muscular build supported by pillar-like legs. Fossil evidence suggests these creatures were highly social, often traveling in herds to protect themselves from predators. Their teeth were specialized for stripping tough vegetation, allowing them to thrive in diverse ecosystems. Despite being less famous than later giants like Brachiosaurus, Patagosaurus played a vital role in the evolution of sauropod diversity, bridging the gap between earlier primitive forms and later behemoths.

attacks.description: The dinosaur swings its powerful tail to strike foes with blunt force.
attacks.name: Tail Smash

attacks.description: Uses its long neck to reach down and trample enemies from above.
attacks.name: Arboreal Stomp

passiveAbility.description: Reduces damage taken from physical attacks by 10% due to its dense, thick hide.
passiveAbility.name: Rugged Hide

stats.name: Polacanthus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to Many Spines because of the protective armor covering its back and hips.
stats.description: Polacanthus was a fascinating armored dinosaur that roamed the Early Cretaceous landscapes of what is now England. This nodosaurid herbivore was characterized by a distinct sacral shield—a solid mass of fused bone protecting its pelvic region—and long, sharp spikes lining its sides. These structural defenses provided excellent protection against the predatory dinosaurs of its time. With a low-slung, sturdy build, it grazed peacefully on low-lying vegetation. Despite its intimidating appearance, the Polacanthus was a gentle giant, relying entirely on its formidable natural armor to discourage attackers while navigating the ancient forests and river deltas it called home.

attacks.name: Tail Swipe Spike Defense: 30 damage, The Polacanthus whips its spiky tail to damage nearby attackers and reduce incoming physical strike power.
Armored Charge: 45 damage, The dinosaur rushes the opponent using its reinforced shoulders to crush defenses.

passiveAbility.name: Natural Armor Plate: Reduces all incoming damage by 15 percent due to the dense, keratinous spikes covering the dinosaur's body.

stats.name: Psittacosaurus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to parrot lizard because of its distinctive beak used for shearing tough plant materials.
stats.description: Psittacosaurus is a genus of extinct ceratopsian dinosaur that thrived across Asia during the Early Cretaceous period. Known for its parrot-like beak, this small bipedal herbivore possessed a specialized set of teeth that allowed it to process tough vegetation. Fossils reveal that some species had long, bristle-like filaments on their tails, suggesting primitive feathers. Despite its modest size, Psittacosaurus was incredibly successful, with numerous species identified across diverse ecosystems. These dinosaurs likely lived in herds to protect themselves from predators, marking them as a significant and fascinating evolutionary link in the history of horned dinosaur development.

attacks.name: Beak Snap Dashout damage: 15, description: Delivers a quick, precise bite to the opponent's ankles.
Tail Bristle Sweep damage: 25, description: Uses its quill-like tail to strike opponents at close range.

passiveAbility.name: Small Stature Evasion, description: Due to its low profile, the dinosaur is harder to target, increasing defense against large-scale attacks.

stats.name: Pyroraptor
stats.funFact: Its name translates to 'fire thief' because it was discovered in the Provence region after a forest fire.
stats.description: The Pyroraptor was a small, agile dromaeosaurid known for its swift movement and lethal sickle-shaped claws on its feet. Thriving in the humid, island-strewn environments of prehistoric Europe, this predator hunted in packs, utilizing advanced coordination and speed to take down larger prey. Its streamlined skeleton and bird-like features suggest it was covered in feathers, likely aiding in both insulation and display. As a member of the raptor family, it represents a remarkable evolutionary branch that highlights the transition between classic theropod dinosaurs and modern avian species, showcasing immense dexterity and intelligence for its time.

attacks.description: Inflicts rapid physical damage infused with a jolting electric shock.
Leaps from above, discharging a bolt of lightning upon impact.

stats.name: Qianzhousaurus
stats.funFact: It is affectionately nicknamed Pinocchio rex due to its unusually long and narrow snout compared to other tyrannosaurs.
stats.description: Qianzhousaurus is a distinctive genus of tyrannosaurid theropod that lived during the Late Cretaceous period. Unlike its robust cousin T-Rex, this predator possessed a significantly more elongated skull, suggesting it occupied a specialized ecological niche. Its lighter frame allowed for greater agility, making it a lethal hunter of smaller, faster prey. Fossil evidence discovered in the Nanxiong Formation reveals a complex evolutionary lineage that challenges previous assumptions about tyrant dinosaur morphology. With its serrated teeth and lightning-fast reflexes, this dinosaur represents a pinnacle of Cretaceous predatory adaptation, blending raw power with specialized anatomical precision in its hunting strategy.

attacks.name: Pinocchio Piercer bite damage: 45 description: A lightning-fast strike from its elongated jaws that pierces through armor.
Static Stalk damage: 30 description: The user channels electricity through its scales, stunning the opponent for one turn.

passiveAbility.name: Conductive Reflexes description: Increases evasion by 15% during rain or storm weather conditions.

stats.name: Scansoriopteryx
stats.funFact: It possessed an unusually long, stiff, and digit-like third finger, which researchers believe was specifically adapted for probing tree bark to extract insect larvae.
stats.description: Scansoriopteryx is a fascinating genus of small, bird-like theropod dinosaurs discovered in the Tiaojishan Formation of China. Often cited as one of the most primitive members of the Avialae lineage, it displays a unique suite of morphological traits including elongated fingers and specialized feathers that suggest an arboreal, or tree-dwelling, lifestyle. Its small stature and agile build allowed it to navigate prehistoric forest canopies with ease. Unlike many of its larger, ground-dwelling cousins, this creature utilized its specialized appendages to forage for insects and seeds, providing critical evidence for the evolutionary transition between terrestrial dinosaurs and true flight-capable birds.

stats.name: Sinosauropteryx
stats.funFact: It was the first non-avian dinosaur discovered with fossilized feathers, which were found to have a reddish-brown banded pattern.
stats.description: Sinosauropteryx is a pivotal species in paleontological history, representing one of the smallest theropods ever discovered. Living during the Early Cretaceous, this delicate creature featured a long tail and soft, downy proto-feathers that provided insulation rather than flight capability. Its slender build allowed for agile movement across the prehistoric landscapes of China. Recent analysis of melanosomes preserved in its fossils has allowed scientists to reconstruct its actual color palette, revealing stripes along its tail. As a small, opportunistic carnivore, it hunted insects and small lizards, occupying a vital niche in the ecosystem before the rise of giant apex predators.

stats.name: Yangchuanosaurus
stats.funFact: This predator possessed a unique, rugose texture on its snout, likely used for display during courtship or territorial disputes.
stats.description: Yangchuanosaurus was a formidable apex predator that roamed the lush forests of the Late Jurassic period in present-day China. As a member of the metriacanthosaurid family, this theropod was characterized by its massive skull, serrated teeth, and powerful, muscular limbs. It hunted diverse prey, likely targeting large sauropods that shared its environment. With a lean build designed for agility and surprising speed, it maintained its position as a dominant carnivore within its ecosystem. Its fossil remains provide paleontologists with critical insights into the evolutionary history and anatomical complexity of Asian carnivorous dinosaurs, highlighting the vibrant biodiversity of the Jurassic era.

attacks.name: Serrated Slash: Nature's Edge 1800-Year-Old Bark Cleave

passiveAbility.name: Photosynthetic Stealth: Camouflaged in Leafy Shadows

stats.name: Yutyrannus
stats.funFact: Yutyrannus is currently the largest dinosaur species known to have preserved evidence of feathers, making it a giant fluffy predator.
stats.description: Yutyrannus, whose name means beautiful feathered tyrant, represents a massive leap in our understanding of theropod evolution. Unlike its later, scaled relatives, this apex predator possessed a thick coat of filamentous proto-feathers, likely used for insulation in the cool Early Cretaceous climate of China. Despite its fluffy appearance, it was a formidable hunter, dominating its environment with powerful jaws and sharp teeth. Its discovery challenged long-held assumptions that giant theropods were strictly reptilian in appearance, proving that even the most terrifying predators of the ancient world could have been covered in diverse, bird-like plumage throughout their lives.

stats.name: Abelisaurus
stats.funFact: The skull of the Abelisaurus is unusually large and lacks the horns seen in some of its later relatives like Carnotaurus.
stats.description: Abelisaurus was a formidable theropod that patrolled the landscapes of South America during the Late Cretaceous period. Known for its distinctively long, deep skull, it was a specialized hunter of its time. Although it lacked the intricate horns or armor found in other abelisaurids, its sheer size and powerful jaws made it a top-tier predator. Paleontologists identify this dinosaur as a key link in understanding the evolution of the Abelisauridae family. With its aggressive stance and fiery nature, it dominates the battlefield, using its incredible bite force to dismantle rivals and maintain its reputation as a fearsome apex predator.

attacks.description: The dinosaur lunges forward with a searing, fiery bite that leaves the opponent scorched.
attacks.name: Inferno Bite

attacks.description: A rapid series of stomps that ignites the ground, damaging enemies within a small radius.
attacks.name: Magma Stomp

passiveAbility.description: Increases damage output by 20% when the health falls below 30%.
passiveAbility.name: Burning Vengeance

stats.name: Sauroposeidon
stats.funFact: Its name translates to Lizard Earthquake God, honoring the Greek god Poseidon, because it was likely strong enough to cause localized tremors with every step.
stats.description: Sauroposeidon stands as one of the tallest dinosaurs to have ever walked the Earth, possessing an incredibly long neck that allowed it to reach vegetation high in the canopy. This massive sauropod thrived in the humid environments of Early Cretaceous North America. Due to its sheer stature, it had few natural predators, using its immense size and whip-like tail as defensive measures. Its skeletal structure was surprisingly lightweight for its size, allowing it to support such a long neck while grazing effectively. Today, it remains an iconic symbol of the titanic giants that dominated prehistoric ecosystems across the continent.

stats.name: Stegosaurus
stats.funFact: The brain of a Stegosaurus was only the size of a walnut, one of the smallest relative to body size of any dinosaur.
stats.description: Stegosaurus is an iconic armored herbivore characterized by its double row of kite-shaped plates along its back and a spiked tail known as a thagomizer. This gentle giant walked on all fours, grazing on low-lying vegetation in North America during the Late Jurassic. Its plates likely served for thermoregulation or display purposes, while the tail spikes provided a formidable defense against predators like Allosaurus. Despite its intimidating appearance, this dinosaur possessed a surprisingly small brain, suggesting simple behaviors. Its unique silhouette remains one of the most recognizable in paleontology, representing a fascinating evolutionary adaptation to its lush, prehistoric environment.

attacks.name: Tidal Thagomizer
attacks.description: Swings its spiked tail to unleash a powerful blast of pressurized water at the opponent.

attacks.name: Aquatic Plate Shield
attacks.description: The back plates absorb ambient moisture to create a protective water veil that deflects damage.

passiveAbility.name: Hydrated Defense
passiveAbility.description: Stegosaurus regains 5 HP every turn while standing on terrain with water sources.

stats.name: Ouranosaurus
stats.funFact: Ouranosaurus is famous for the large, sail-like structure on its back, which may have been used for temperature regulation or signaling.
stats.description: Ouranosaurus was a large iguanodontian dinosaur that lived during the Early Cretaceous period. Distinguishable by the long, high neural spines on its back, this herbivore likely possessed a sail or a fatty hump similar to a modern camel. Its flat, duck-like bill was perfectly adapted for grazing on low-lying vegetation. While primarily terrestrial, it maintained a robust posture. As a psychic-attuned creature in this context, it possesses a heightened awareness of its surroundings, allowing it to sense threats long before they approach. Despite its peaceful foraging nature, its sheer size and structural evolution make it a formidable prehistoric presence.

attacks.name: Mental Grazing
attacks.description: The user taps into the psychic plane to calm the target while simultaneously draining energy.

attacks.name: Sail Radiance
attacks.description: The spines on the back glow with psychic energy to release a concentrated shockwave.

passiveAbility.name: Thermal Foresight
passiveAbility.description: The user anticipates attacks based on thermal patterns, granting a 15% increase in dodge chance.

stats.name: Shunosaurus
stats.funFact: Shunosaurus is one of the few known sauropods to possess a defensive club at the end of its tail, equipped with two bony spikes.
stats.description: Shunosaurus was a medium-sized sauropod that roamed what is now China during the Middle Jurassic period. Unlike many of its larger long-necked relatives, this dinosaur was relatively compact, reaching about 10 meters in total length. Its most distinctive feature was the heavy, spiked tail club, which served as a formidable deterrent against predatory theropods. Scientists believe this armor evolved as a direct response to the increasing pressure from predators within its ecosystem. Despite its sturdy build, Shunosaurus lived in lush river environments, browsing on soft vegetation while utilizing its unique tail for protection in territorial disputes.

attacks.name: Spiked Club Slam
attacks.description: The Shunosaurus swings its tail with intense heat, dealing physical damage plus additional fire impact.

attacks.name: Blazing Tail Whip
attacks.description: Ignites the tip of the tail to strike enemies with a searing arc of fire.

passiveAbility.name: Thermal Defense
passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming damage from opponents while the dinosaur is within a volcanic biome.

stats.name: Edmontosaurus
stats.funFact: Edmontosaurus possessed a fleshy comb on its head, similar to a rooster, which may have been used for display purposes.
stats.description: Edmontosaurus was a massive, duck-billed dinosaur that roamed the plains of North America during the late Cretaceous period. As a highly successful hadrosaur, it traveled in large social herds to evade predators. With a wide, flat beak perfect for grazing on tough vegetation and a complex arrangement of grinding teeth, it was an efficient herbivore. Recent soft tissue discoveries reveal it likely had a prominent fleshy crest on its head. Though lacking defensive horns or spikes, its sheer size, speed, and herd instincts made it a dominant herbivore that shaped the landscape of its ancient ecosystem for millions of years.

attacks.name: Static Stomp
attacks.description: The Edmontosaurus slams its feet, sending a jolt of lightning surging through the ground at enemies.

attacks.name: Herd Charge
attacks.description: A powerful coordinated charge that builds up high-voltage kinetic energy upon impact.

passiveAbility.name: Storm Aura
passiveAbility.description: Every time this dinosaur is attacked, it builds up static charge, increasing its next attack damage by 10%.

stats.name: Dreadnoughtus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to 'fears nothing' due to its massive size and lack of natural predators in adulthood.
stats.description: Dreadnoughtus is one of the largest terrestrial animals to have ever walked the Earth. This gargantuan titanosaurian sauropod possessed a long neck and a whip-like tail that served as a defensive weapon. Despite its immense stature, it was a gentle herbivore that spent its days grazing on high-canopy vegetation in what is now Argentina. Discovered in 2005, the skeletal remains provided unprecedented insights into sauropod anatomy and growth rates. Its immense weight and sheer physical presence made it virtually invulnerable to contemporary predators, allowing it to roam its environment with complete dominance during the Cretaceous period.

attacks.name: Tidal Tail Whip
attacks.description: Swings its massive, water-infused tail to create a shockwave of crushing aquatic force.

attacks.name: Abyssal Stomp
attacks.description: Delivers a heavy ground-pounding stomp that triggers geyser-like explosions beneath the opponent.

passiveAbility.name: Hydro-Massive Armor
passiveAbility.description: Absorbs surrounding water molecules to fortify its skin, reducing incoming damage by 20 percent.

stats.name: Einiosaurus
stats.funFact: The name Einiosaurus comes from the Blackfeet word for bison and Greek for lizard.
stats.description: Einiosaurus is a distinctive genus of centrosaurine ceratopsid dinosaur known for its unique cranial ornamentation. Unlike its close relatives, this herbivore possessed a single, strongly forward-curved horn on its nose, which resembled a hook. It also featured two large spikes projecting backward from its neck frill. Living in large herds across the plains of modern-day Montana, Einiosaurus likely utilized these specialized horns for intraspecific display and defense against apex predators. Its robust build and beak-like mouth were perfectly adapted for grazing on low-lying prehistoric vegetation in a changing environment during the Late Cretaceous period.

attacks.name: Hooked Charge
attacks.description: The Einiosaurus lowers its head and charges forward using its signature curved nose horn to pierce through opponent armor.

attacks.name: Frill Bash
attacks.description: The dinosaur aggressively swings its neck frill at the target, using its sharp spikes to deal heavy physical impact damage.

passiveAbility.name: Herd Protection
passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming damage by 15% when fighting in grassy environments due to camouflage and defensive posturing.

stats.name: Pentaceratops
stats.funFact: Its name means five-horned face, referring to its two brow horns, one nose horn, and two prominent cheek bones.
stats.description: Pentaceratops was a massive ceratopsid dinosaur known for its incredibly large skull, which is among the largest of any terrestrial vertebrate. Roaming the coastal plains of ancient North America roughly 75 million years ago, this herbivore was built like a living tank. Its bony neck frill provided protection against predators, while its sturdy frame allowed it to forage through thick vegetation with ease. Today, it remains an iconic figure in paleontology, representing the sheer scale and diversity of horned dinosaurs that thrived during the final stages of the Age of Reptiles before their eventual extinction event.

attacks.name: Magma Gore
attacks.description: A powerful charge that inflicts heavy damage and leaves the opponent scorched.

attacks.name: Embershield Bash
attacks.description: Uses its large frill to strike foes while emitting searing heat waves.

passiveAbility.name: Thermal Resilience
passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming damage from water and ice-based attacks by 20 percent.

stats.name: Titanosaurus
stats.funFact: Despite its name, it is actually a relatively small titanosaur compared to its larger cousins like Argentinosaurus.
stats.description: Titanosaurus was a titanosaurian sauropod that roamed the regions of modern-day India during the Late Cretaceous period. Characterized by its long neck, slender tail, and robust body, this massive herbivore browsed on high-reaching vegetation. Though its fossils were once used to categorize a wider group of similar dinosaurs, modern science now recognizes it as a distinct, moderately sized giant. Its body was covered in osteoderms, providing a bony layer of natural armor against the sharp-toothed predators of its era. This impressive creature represents the incredible evolutionary success of the sauropod lineage surviving until the end of the dinosaur age.

attacks.name: Thunder Stomp
attacks.description: Slams the ground with massive legs, sending a shockwave of electrical energy through the earth.

attacks.name: Tail Whiplash
attacks.description: A swift, electrified strike with its long tail that causes paralysis and physical trauma.

passiveAbility.name: Static Hide
passiveAbility.description: Any melee attacker has a chance to be stunned by the static electrical field emanating from its armored back.

stats.name: Suzhousaurus
stats.funFact: Its name translates to Su-zhou lizard in honor of the region where it was discovered.
stats.description: Suzhousaurus is a fascinating therizinosauroid from the Early Cretaceous period of China. Unlike most theropods that were active predators, this creature possessed a bulky, barrel-shaped body and long, sickle-like claws adapted for foraging through dense prehistoric vegetation. It stands out in the evolutionary record for its distinct morphology, representing an intermediate stage in the development of the highly specialized Therizinosauridae family. Its unique anatomy suggests it was a slow-moving browser that used its formidable reach to pull down branches. This dinosaur provides critical insight into the diverse ecological roles filled by theropods during the Mesozoic era.

attacks.name: Sickle Sweep
attacks.description: Swings its massive forelimb claws in a wide arc to shred through plant barriers and foes.

attacks.name: Forager's Stomp
attacks.description: Uses its heavy body weight to create a localized tremor, hindering the agility of its opponent.

passiveAbility.name: Nature's Camouflage
passiveAbility.description: While standing in tall vegetation, Suzhousaurus gains a defensive boost against incoming physical attacks.

stats.name: Parasaurolophus
stats.funFact: Its long head crest may have functioned as a resonating chamber to produce loud, low-frequency trumpet calls.
stats.description: Imagine wandering through a lush, humid forest in North America around 75 million years ago. You would likely encounter the Parasaurolophus, a unique hadrosaur known for the long, tube-like crest extending from the back of its skull. These dinosaurs were social animals that spent their days grazing on tough conifers and ferns. The environment was warm and vibrant, filled with towering trees and vast swampy floodplains. They probably moved in herds, communicating with each other through resonant sounds that echoed across the landscape. With their powerful back legs, they could walk on all fours or stand tall to reach higher branches, always keeping a watchful eye out for roaming predators while living in these productive, sun-drenched coastal lowlands.

attacks.name: Crest Trumpet
attacks.description: Blasts an incredibly loud sound wave that leaves the opponent stunned and slightly annoyed.

attacks.name: Tail Thwack
attacks.description: Swings its massive tail with surprising speed to remind everyone who owns the feeding ground.

passiveAbility.name: Herd Instinct
passiveAbility.description: Reduces incoming damage by 20 points while other Grass-type dinosaurs are on the field.
